compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin developer, I can select the next sprint batch via the CLI
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `ticket next-batch` subcommand that replaces sprint-next-batch.sh's ~963-line implementation — performs the hierarchy walk, blocking checks, file-overlap serialization, and greedy candidate selection. Preserves the exact output contract (output tokens and --json schema) consumed by write-blackboard.sh and sprint SKILL.md.

**Why**: Moves the core batch-selection logic from a workflow script into the ticket CLI with a stable, versioned contract so the sprint orchestrator can consume it through a CLI boundary rather than coupling to a script's internal evolution.

**Scope**:
- IN: ticket-next-batch.sh (or Python) implementation, dispatcher integration, hierarchy walk, blocking checks, file-overlap serialization, greedy selection, all SKIPPED_* output tokens and --json schema, integration tests (blocking behavior + file-overlap), unit tests
- OUT: Refactoring sprint-next-batch.sh callers (that migration is Epic e1b2-2653's concern); refactoring write-blackboard.sh to consume the new subcommand (also e1b2-2653)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket next-batch` produces the same batch selection as sprint-next-batch.sh given an identical fixture state
  ← Satisfies: "next-batch (batch selection with hierarchy walk, blocking checks, file-overlap serialization, greedy selection — replaces sprint-next-batch.sh/issue-batch.sh; preserves SKIPPED_DESIGN_AWAITING, SKIPPED_BLOCKED_STORY, SKIPPED_NEEDS_PLANNING output tokens and --json format consumed by write-blackboard.sh)"
- When this story is complete, an integration test asserts that a blocked story's child tasks are excluded from the batch
  ← Satisfies: "tests assert correct blocking behavior (blocked story's child tasks excluded from batch)"
- When this story is complete, an integration test asserts that overlapping files produce exactly one SKIPPED_OVERLAP emission
  ← Satisfies: "correct file-overlap serialization (overlapping files produce exactly one SKIPPED_OVERLAP)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Must preserve all SKIPPED_* output tokens emitted by sprint-next-batch.sh (SKIPPED_OVERLAP, SKIPPED_BLOCKED_STORY, SKIPPED_NEEDS_PLANNING, SKIPPED_DESIGN_AWAITING at minimum) and the --json schema fields consumed by write-blackboard.sh
- [Reliability] Verify against the production sprint-next-batch.sh behavior: check for any additional tokens (e.g., SKIPPED_OPUS_CAP, SKIPPED_IN_PROGRESS), OPUS_CAP constant, OVERLAP_SAFE_FILES set, and stderr conflict matrix — treat those as production contract even if not enumerated in the epic SCs
- [Testing] Integration test should follow the pattern of test-sprint-next-batch.sh for fixture setup
- [Reliability] Replaces sprint-next-batch.sh's inline Python BFS — likely depends on ticket list-descendants (story 99aa-ccf0) for tree walk; implement in dependency order to avoid duplicating BFS logic

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update: Preserve all six SKIPPED_* tokens emitted by sprint-next-batch.sh (SKIPPED_OVERLAP, SKIPPED_BLOCKED_STORY, SKIPPED_OPUS_CAP, SKIPPED_IN_PROGRESS, SKIPPED_NEEDS_PLANNING, SKIPPED_DESIGN_AWAITING) — not only the four listed in the epic SCs. Preserve EPIC:, AVAILABLE_POOL:, BATCH_SIZE:, TASK: line formats. Preserve the full --json output schema (available_pool, opus_cap, skipped_overlap, skipped_opus_cap, skipped_blocked_story, skipped_design_awaiting, skipped_in_progress, skipped_needs_planning) consumed by write-blackboard.sh and sprint SKILL.md Phase 3/5. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability] next-batch must preserve (beyond the SKIPPED_* tokens listed in the epic SCs): (1) the OPUS_CAP=2 constant and SKIPPED_OPUS_CAP emission (controls usage-aware throttling — removing it blows the opus dispatch cap); (2) the OVERLAP_SAFE_FILES set (currently .test-index and similar shared-by-design files that MUST NOT trigger file-overlap serialization); (3) the human-readable conflict matrix printed to stderr when >=2 candidates contend for overlapping files (primary debugging tool for 'why was my batch serialized'). None of these are enumerated in the epic success criteria, but all three are production behavior of sprint-next-batch.sh and their omission would cause silent regressions. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude
